key_insight: Plasma-sprayed Inconel 625 on T22 steel initially resists nitrate-salt corrosion, but prolonged exposure at 500 °C leads to progressive coating degradation and eventual penetration toward the substrate interface.
engineering_relevance: For CSP thermal energy storage components, In625 spray coatings can delay corrosion of carbon steel, but their long-term durability in molten nitrate salts is limited and must be validated before deployment.
method: Compact Plasma Spray deposited In625 onto T22 carbon steel.
Samples were exposed to eutectic nitrate salt at 500 °C.
Corrosion was assessed after 24, 72, 168, 336, and 504 h.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports thin surface-localized corrosion products after 24 h, then increasing through-thickness attack over time, reaching the coating/substrate interface by about 500 h and a corrosion rate of 0.4 mm/year.
watch_item: Low cohesion between sprayed splats may accelerate corrosion and undermine coating integrity under long-term high-temperature service.
paper_title: Corrosion Behavior of Plasma-Sprayed In625 Coatings Exposed to Nitrate Salts in Concentrating Solar Power Plants
